consequence.description: Without reserve adequacy methods, the system may understate required capital reserves for insurance or pension liabilities, leading to regulatory violations and potential insolvency risk

core.when: When processing reserve adequacy calculations in data_acquisition
core.action: Integrate external reserve adequacy calculation service: pass policy_data and assumption_data fields to certified reserve calculator, and validate output against regulatory minimum thresholds (reserve >= best_estimate * risk_margin_multiplier)

consequence.description: Without proper reserve adequacy validation, insufficient reserves may be reported to regulators, causing compliance violations and potential fines or license revocation

core.when: When implementing or modifying data pipeline stages in data_acquisition
core.action: Assume the framework handles mortality table version management — the framework does not track or validate mortality table versions; stale or mismatched mortality tables may be used without warning

consequence.description: Using incorrect mortality table version causes systematic miscalculation of life expectancy and death probabilities, leading to reserves being overstated or understated by 5-15% for long-duration policies

core.action: Implement mortality table version control: store table version in metadata, validate version matches regulatory filing version, and reject calculations with version mismatch (version field must equal approved_version from regulatory registry)

consequence.description: Using incorrect mortality table version causes systematic miscalculation of life expectancy and death probabilities, leading to reserves being overstated or understated by 5-15% for long-duration policies
